calendar.matters:
PETITIONER ORLANDO BEACH PIZZA, INC., DBA DOMINO'S PIZZA'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITION APPEARANCE OF STRAVA, INC. (TENTATIVE RULING PART 1 OF 2)
Off Calendar Nov-08-2024 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition Appearance Of Strava, Inc.; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion
Off Calendar Mar-10-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Atty. DAVID KEMPEN, Esq. of Riley Safer Holmes & Cancila LLP (tel: 415-275-8550) for Petitioner Orlando Beach Pizza, Inc.
Atty. MICHAEL BLEICHER, Esq. of Zwillgen Law LLP (tel: 202-296-3585) for Non-party Strava, Inc (via Zoom videoconference).

Hearing Re: PETITIONER ORLANDO BEACH PIZZA, INC., DBA DOMINO'S PIZZA's Motion To Compel Deposition Appearance Of Strava, Inc.

Petitioner contests the tentative ruling. Both parties stipulate to the Judge Pro Tem. Petitioner's counsel signs the stipulation. Non-party Strava, Inc.'s counsel authorizes the clerk to sign the stipulation on his behalf. Matter argued and submitted. Upon conclusion, the Court orders as follows: Counsel for petitioner is to provide counsel for Non-party Strava, Inc. with a list of questions or points in accordance with point 1 of the tentative ruling, by no later than 11/11/2024. Counsel for non-party Strava, Inc. is to respond by 11/18/2024, providing Petitioner's counsel with a sworn declaration from Strava, Inc.'s PMQ or PMK. Counsel for petitioner is to email Judge Pro Tem Philip Ward after receiving the declaration, informing the Court as to whether as deposition is still needed. The Court takes the matter under submission.

Judge Pro Tem: Philip Ward; Clerk: V. da Fonseca; Court reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR 13293 (tel: 415-274-9977 / email: calendar-LA@veritext.com). (525/JPT)
